message.content:
Search the HQ codebase for code that should be connected to real data but isn't. Look for:
1. Components receiving hardcoded props instead of dynamic data
2. State that's initialized with static values instead of fetched data
3. Functions that return static data instead of calling APIs/CLI
4. invoke() calls that are missing or commented out
5. Features that show "No data" or empty states when they should have real data

Focus on how data flows from:
- harness CLI commands → React components
- Tauri invoke → state updates
- Project selection → data fetching

Report what's broken and what needs to be connected.

content.text: I'll search the HQ codebase to identify data connection issues. Let me start by understanding the current structure and then systematically find disconnected data flows.

  {
    "id": "F012",
    "description": "Native UX Polish - Make the app feel like a native macOS application, not a web app",
    "category": "ux",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "estimatedEffort": "9 hours",
    "platform": "macOS only",
    "steps": [
      "Phase 0: Native Menu Bar - Create macOS-style menu (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help) in src-tauri/src/lib.rs using Tauri Menu API",

      "phase0_native_menu": {
        "description": "Native Menu Bar (macOS style)",
        "effort": "1 hour",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "problem": "Current menu looks like web app (HQ, Edit...), not native macOS",
          "solution": "Use Tauri Menu API with PredefinedMenuItem for standard macOS menus",
          "menus": ["HQ (About, Settings, Services, Hide, Quit)", "File (Open Project, New Tab, Close Tab)", "Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All)", "View (Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Logs, Fullscreen)", "Window (Minimize, Maximize, Prev/Next Tab)", "Help (Documentation, GitHub)"]
        }

        "description": "CSS Foundation (No dependencies)",
        "effort": "30 min",
        "files": ["src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "font": "ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system at 14px (native apps use 13-14px, not 16px)",
          "userSelect": "none on all UI elements, text only on content (.selectable, pre, code, .log-content)",

          "focus": ":focus { outline: none } + :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ... }",

          "overscroll": "overscroll-behavior: none to prevent bounce",

        "description": "Disable Browser Behaviors",

          "devMode": "Keep DEV_TOOLS and RELOAD enabled in debug mode",
          "usage": "tauri_plugin_prevent_default::Builder::new().with_flags(Flags::all() - Flags::DEV_TOOLS - Flags::RELOAD).build()"
        }
      },

        "description": "Window Vibrancy (macOS blur effect)",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],

            "windows": [{ "transparent": true, "decorations": true, "titleBarStyle": "Overlay", "hiddenTitle": true }]
          },
          "rustCode": "apply_vibrancy(&window, NSVisualEffectMaterial::Sidebar, None, None)",
          "materials": ["Sidebar (recommended)", "HudWindow (darker)", "FullScreenUI", "ContentBackground"],
          "limitation": "macOS only, Windows/Linux will have solid background"
        }

          "inspiration": "Notion, Linear - custom rendered menus, not OS native"
        }
      },
      "phase5_keybindings": {
        "description": "Configurable Keybindings (VSCode-style)",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["src/config/defaultKeybindings.json", "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts", "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts"],
        "details": {
          "format": "VSCode keybindings.json: { key, command, when?, args? }",
          "defaultShortcuts": {
            "cmd+t": "tab.new",
            "cmd+w": "tab.close",
            "cmd+shift+[": "tab.previous",
            "cmd+shift+]": "tab.next",
            "cmd+,": "settings.open",
            "cmd+k": "commandPalette.open",
            "cmd+\\": "sidebar.toggle",
            "cmd+j": "logs.toggle",
            "cmd+o": "project.open",
            "escape": "modal.close (when: modalOpen)"
          },
          "userConfig": "~/.config/hq/keybindings.json for user overrides",
          "features": ["Command registry pattern", "When clause for context-aware shortcuts", "Hot-reload on file change", "Merge user overrides with defaults"]
        }
      },
      "phase6_animations": {
        "description": "Spring Animations",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["package.json", "src/components/*.tsx"],
        "details": {
          "library": "motion (formerly Framer Motion)",
          "install": "bun add motion",
          "animations": {
            "tabSwitch": "spring with stiffness: 400, damping: 30",
            "cardExpand": "type: spring, bounce: 0.2",
            "panelResize": "type: tween, ease: easeOut",
            "fadeIn": "initial: { opacity: 0, y: 10 }, animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }"
          },
          "why": "Physical spring physics feel native, not 'webby' easing curves"
        }
      },
      "phase7_focus_blur": {
        "description": "Focus/Blur Window State",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs", "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "rustEvent": "window.on_window_event for Focused(bool), emit 'window-focus' to frontend",
          "frontendHook": "useWindowFocus() returns isFocused, adds .window-unfocused class to html",
          "cssEffect": "Dim colors when unfocused: .window-unfocused .tab-active { background: gray }",
          "inspiration": "Native macOS apps dim selection highlight when window loses focus"
        }
      }
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "rust": ["window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"", "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\""],
      "npm": ["motion"]
    },
    "filesToCreate": [
      "src/config/defaultKeybindings.json",
      "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts",
      "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts",
      "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx"
    ],
    "filesToModify": [
      "src-tauri/Cargo.toml",
      "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json",
      "src-tauri/src/lib.rs",
      "src/index.css",
      "package.json"

      "https://github.com/ferreira-tb/tauri-plugin-prevent-default",
      "https://getlotus.app/21-making-electron-apps-feel-native-on-mac",
      "https://motion.dev/"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "platform": "macOS only for this implementation",
      "inspiration": ["Linear", "Notion", "Warp", "Arc", "Raycast", "VSCode"],
      "knownLimitations": [
        "Vibrancy is macOS-only",

        "DevTools kept in dev mode for debugging"
      ]
    }
  },

   240→================================================================================
   241→TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
   242→================================================================================
   243→
   244→## Problem Statement
   245→
   246→Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
   247→(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).
   248→
   249→When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
   250→which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:
   251→
   252→### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
   253→- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
   254→- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
   255→- File system operations (read/write project files)
   256→- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
   257→- System notifications
   258→- Any Rust backend functionality
   259→
   260→### What DOES work in browser mode:
   261→- React components and UI rendering
   262→- State management (@zazcart/tools)
   263→- Mock data and simulated interactions
   264→- PatternFly components
   265→- Pure frontend logic
   266→
   267→## Why This Matters
   268→
   269→1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
   270→2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
   271→3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality
   272→
   273→## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge
   274→
   275→Create a development-only RPC bridge that:
   276→
   277→```
   278→┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
   279→│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
   280→│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
   281→│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
   282→│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
   283→│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
   284→└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
   285→```
   286→
   287→### Architecture
   288→
   289→1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   290→   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   291→   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   292→   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   293→   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)
   294→
   295→2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   296→   ```typescript
   297→   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   298→   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
   299→     if (isTauri()) {
   300→       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
   301→     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
   302→       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
   303→     } else {
   304→       throw new Error('No backend available');
   305→     }
   306→   }
   307→   ```
   308→
   309→3. **Security Measures**:
   310→   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   311→   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   312→   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   313→   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   314→   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)
   315→
   316→### Future SDK Consideration
   317→
   318→The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
   319→- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
   320→- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
   321→- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
   322→- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer
   323→
   324→**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
   325→design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.
   326→
   327→## Implementation Steps
   328→
   329→See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
================================================================================

## Problem Statement

Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).

When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:

### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
- File system operations (read/write project files)
- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
- System notifications
- Any Rust backend functionality

### What DOES work in browser mode:
- React components and UI rendering
- State management (@zazcart/tools)
- Mock data and simulated interactions
- PatternFly components
- Pure frontend logic

## Why This Matters

1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality

## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge

Create a development-only RPC bridge that:

```
┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
```

### Architecture

1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)

2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   ```typescript
   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
     if (isTauri()) {
       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
     } else {
       throw new Error('No backend available');
     }
   }
   ```

3. **Security Measures**:
   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)

### Future SDK Consideration

The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer

**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.

## Implementation Steps

See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

## CRITICAL ANALYSIS: Data Connection Issues in HQ Codebase

Based on my thorough search of the HQ React/Tauri application, I've identified multiple critical data flow disconnections. Here's what's broken and what needs to be connected:

---

## 1. KANBAN BOARD - Completely Disconnected from Real Data

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/App.tsx` (lines 431)

**Problem:**
```typescript
<KanbanBoard features={MOCK_FEATURES} />
```

**Issue:** The Kanban board receives hardcoded mock features (lines 121-213) instead of fetching from the database via the harness CLI. The mock data has `passes: false` for F003 and F004 even though progress.txt shows these are completed (F003 and F004 are both marked as `passes: true` in features.json).

**What should happen:**
- App should call `run_harness_command(['task', 'list', '--format', 'json'])` to fetch tasks from the harness DB
- Parse the JSON response
- Pass real features to KanbanBoard
- Component should re-fetch when file watcher detects changes (via useHarnessWatcher)

**Missing:**
1. No `useEffect` hook in App to fetch features on project change
2. No state management for features (should use @zazcart/tools)
3. No connection to harness CLI API
4. Features aren't persisted to or loaded from the harness database

---

## 2. LOG PANEL - Hardcoded Mock Processes with Static Data

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/App.tsx` (lines 36-118)

**Problem:**
```typescript
const MOCK_PROCESSES = [
  { id: 'dev-server', name: 'Dev Server' },
  { id: 'tauri', name: 'Tauri' },
  { id: 'typescript', name: 'TypeScript' },
];

const createMockLogs = (): LogEntry[] => {
  // ... hardcoded log entries with timestamps from 5000ms ago
};
```

**Issue:** Logs are generated once on component mount and never updated. They simulate dev server output but don't actually capture real process output.

**What should happen:**
- PTY processes should emit `pty-output` events via Tauri
- TerminalPanel and other running processes should emit output
- LogPanel should subscribe to these events in real-time
- Log entries should flow from processes → state → UI

**Missing:**
1. No event listeners for `pty-output` events
2. No state synchronization in @zazcart/tools logsSlice
3. Processes array is static (should come from processes state slice)
4. No real process spawning when user clicks "Start Shell"

---

## 3. PROGRESS CARD - Disabled File Watcher Loop

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/ProgressCard.tsx` (lines 207-212)

**Problem:**
```typescript
// Auto-refresh when .harness files change (F008)
// TEMPORARILY DISABLED - causing loop, needs investigation
// useHarnessWatcher({
//   projectPath,
//   onChange: fetchData,
//   enabled: !!projectPath,
// });
```

**Issue:** The file watcher hook is commented out because it was causing an infinite loop. Without it, the Progress Card doesn't auto-update when the harness database changes.

**Root cause:** The watcher hook likely has a dependency array issue causing it to re-subscribe on every render.

**Missing:**
1. Need to fix the infinite loop in useHarnessWatcher (check dependency array)
2. Re-enable the watcher after fixing
3. Ensure `fetchData` is stable (wrapped in `useCallback` with proper dependencies)

---

## 4. TERMINAL PANEL - RPC Calls Work But No Integration

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/TerminalPanel.tsx`

**Status:** This component is actually well-connected:
- ✅ Uses unified RPC client (isTauri → Tauri invoke, else → Dev Bridge)
- ✅ Listens for PTY output/exit events
- ✅ Spawns processes correctly
- ✅ Handles backend detection

**BUT:** The output is only stored in local component state `output: string[]`. It's not being sent to the global state's logsSlice, so LogPanel can't see this terminal output.

**What should happen:**
- When PTY output arrives, dispatch to state: `dispatch(logsSlice.append(logEntry))`
- This allows LogPanel to show terminal output with filtering/search

---

## 5. SCREENSHOT VIEWER - Partially Connected

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/ScreenshotViewer.tsx`

**Status:** Mostly good:
- ✅ Fetches screenshots via harness CLI
- ✅ Handles loading/empty states
- ✅ Shows gallery and lightbox

**BUT:** 
- Component is never instantiated in App.tsx
- No way for users to add screenshots (no UI integration)
- Would need to be added to a task detail view (which doesn't exist yet)

---

## 6. SETTINGS PANEL - Data Flows But Not Applied

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/Settings.tsx`

**Issue:** Settings are stored in localStorage and passed via state callback, but:
1. Not persisted to harness database
2. Not used by other components (e.g., log buffer size not enforced on LogPanel)
3. Terminal doesn't respect `defaultShell` setting
4. `ptyRows`/`ptyCols` settings not used when spawning PTY

---

## 7. @zazcart/tools State Machine - Unused

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/state/store.ts`

**Problem:** A complete state machine is defined with slices for:
- ui, projects, processes, logs, features

**BUT:** It's never actually used in App.tsx. The progress notes mention this has type issues with @zazcart/tools hooks and App.tsx uses useState instead.

**What should happen:**
1. Fix @zazcart/tools type issues
2. Use the state machine as source of truth
3. Dispatch actions when data changes (harness CLI results, file watcher events, PTY output)
4. Subscribe to state in components

---

## 8. PROJECT SELECTION - Works But Not Connected to Data Loading

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx`

**Status:** Dialog works well:
- ✅ Uses Tauri file picker
- ✅ Validates .harness folders
- ✅ Stores recent projects in localStorage

**BUT:** When a project is selected, the App doesn't automatically load its data:
- No call to `harness status --json`
- No call to `harness task list --json`
- No call to `harness session current --json`

---

## 9. TAB REORDERING - Drag Console Logging Only

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/HQ/src/components/TabBar.tsx` (lines 74-75)

**Problem:**
```typescript
// TODO: Implement reorder when connected to state
console.log('Reorder:', sourceId, '->', targetId);
```

**Missing:** Doesn't actually call the reorder handler. The `onReorder` prop is passed but not used.

---

## DATA FLOW DIAGRAM: What's Broken

```
Harness CLI ─→ (not called)
    ↓
Database at .harness/harness.db
    ↓
run_harness_command (Tauri) ──→ (only called in ProgressCard)
    ↓
JSON Response ──→ (not parsed in KanbanBoard, LogPanel, etc.)
    ↓
@zazcart/tools Store ──→ (defined but UNUSED)
    ↓
React Components ─→ (use local useState + MOCK_DATA instead)
    ↓
User sees OUTDATED/STATIC data
```

---

## PRIORITY FIXES NEEDED

| Priority | Component | Issue | Impact |
|----------|-----------|-------|--------|
| **CRITICAL** | App.tsx | Use mock features instead of fetching from harness DB | Kanban board shows wrong feature status |
| **CRITICAL** | ProgressCard | File watcher disabled (infinite loop) | No auto-refresh when project changes |
| **CRITICAL** | @zazcart/tools | State machine defined but never used | App state scattered across useState hooks |
| **HIGH** | TerminalPanel | Output not synced to global logs state | LogPanel can't see terminal output |
| **HIGH** | LogPanel | Receives static mock data, not real process output | Can't see actual development logs |
| **MEDIUM** | ProjectSelector | Doesn't load project data after selection | Users see empty boards after opening a project |
| **MEDIUM** | Settings | Changes not applied to other components | Settings panel is decorative |
| **MEDIUM** | ScreenshotViewer | Component never instantiated | Feature exists but inaccessible |
| **LOW** | TabBar | Reorder handler not implemented | Drag-to-reorder doesn't work |

---

## SUMMARY

The HQ app is **functionally feature-complete at the UI level** but **critically disconnected from real data**. Components are well-designed but receive mock/hardcoded data instead of fetching from the harness CLI/database. The state management layer (@zazcart/tools) exists but is unused. The file watcher for auto-refresh is disabled due to a bug.

**The core pattern is broken:** Components should fetch from `harness` CLI via Tauri invoke → parse JSON → store in @zazcart/tools state → components read from state + subscribe to file watcher events for updates.

Currently: Components are isolated UI islands showing mock data.
